---
title: "One source, three manuscripts — HTML, PDF & Word"
description: "The same .qmd renders to a web page, a typeset LaTeX PDF and a Microsoft Word document a co-author can track-change."
image: thumbnail.svg
image-alt: "One source, three manuscripts — HTML, PDF & Word — Blood Doctor · Quarto"
date: "2026-07-15"
categories: [Documents]
engine: markdown
---

The oldest friction in academic writing is format juggling: the journal wants a
Word file, your co-author wants a PDF, the pre-print server wants HTML. Quarto
produces **all three from one source**.

## Declare the formats, render once

````yaml
---
title: "A reproducible haematology manuscript"
format:
  html:  default
  pdf:   { documentclass: scrartcl }
  docx:  default
---
````

Then:

````bash
quarto render manuscript.qmd            # builds every format
quarto render manuscript.qmd --to docx  # or just one
````

Cross-references (`@fig-`, `@tbl-`), citations from a `.bib` file, and figure
numbering all travel across formats automatically. Send the **Word** file to a
co-author for track-changes; submit the **PDF** to the journal; post the **HTML**
as a pre-print — never re-formatting by hand.

::: {.callout-tip}
## Open the rendered outputs
[HTML](/outputs/manuscript.html) · [**PDF**](/outputs/manuscript.pdf) ·
[**Word (.docx)**](/outputs/manuscript.docx) — all three built from the one file.
:::
